# Python — 2024 Update

Python 2 reached its official end of life on January 1, 2020, and is no longer
maintained. Python 3 is now the only supported major version of the language.

Recent releases have continued to improve performance. Python 3.11, released in
October 2022, delivered significant speed improvements over Python 3.10. The
language is now governed by a Steering Council, which replaced Guido van Rossum's
former role as Benevolent Dictator For Life (BDFL) after he stepped down from
that position in 2018.

Python has become one of the most widely used languages for data science,
machine learning, and scientific computing.
